claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 10 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK

Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin. To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your child.

**First question: How old is your child?**

This helps me determine which eligibility pathway applies:
- Children under 16 generally qualify automatically (with some conditions)
- Children aged 16-19 may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children aged 20 or over typically don't qualify

Please tell me your child's age.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Thanks for helping me with this. I actually have two children I'd like to ask about - Alex, who is 10, and Blake, who is 2. Can we check if I'm eligible for both of them?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! Yes, absolutely - I can help you check eligibility for both Alex (age 10) and Blake (age 2).

Since both of your children are under 16, they fall into the category that generally qualifies automatically, but I need to check a few conditions for each of them.

Let me start with Alex, who is 10 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question about Alex (age 10):**

Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?

At age 10, it's very unlikely Alex would be in paid work, but I need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - they're only 10, so they're just at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that makes sense! Alex is just at school and not in paid work.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

Again, at age 10 this is unlikely, but I need to confirm that Alex is NOT receiving these types of benefits.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship program?

(At age 10, this would be very unusual as apprenticeships typically start at age 16 or later, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship - they're only 10, so they're just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That's exactly what I expected. Alex is just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask about **your residency status** as the claimant. This applies to your eligibility for both Alex and Blake.

**Residency question:**

Do you normally live in the UK? 

Or if not, do any of these special circumstances apply to you:
- You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You have pre-settled status and are working, studying, or job-seeking
- You're a Crown servant living abroad
- Other specific qualifying circumstances

Could you tell me about your residency situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't currently live in the UK. I live abroad and none of those special circumstances apply to me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for letting me know. Unfortunately, this is an important requirement for Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake**.

**The reason:** You do not meet the UK residency requirements.

**What this means:**

To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, OR
- Meet one of the special circumstances such as:
  - Having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
  - Having pre-settled status and meeting additional criteria (working, studying, job-seeking, or having sufficient resources)
  - Being a Crown servant living abroad
  - Living in certain qualifying countries

Since you live abroad and none of these special circumstances apply to you, unfortunately you cannot claim Child Benefit at this time - even though both Alex (age 10) and Blake (age 2) would otherwise qualify based on their ages.

**What you might consider:**

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move back to the UK or obtain settled/pre-settled status), you could reapply for Child Benefit at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or these residency requirements?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
I understand. Thank you for explaining that to me. So even though both my children would otherwise qualify, because I don't live in the UK, I'm not eligible to claim Child Benefit. That's clear now.

<invoke_tool>
<tool_name>exit_loop</tool_name>
<parameters>
</parameters>
</invoke_tool>

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: You're exactly right, and I'm glad the explanation was clear. Let me provide you with the final assessment for your records.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final Assessment Summary:**

**For Alex (age 10):** Not eligible
- Alex meets all the child-specific criteria (right age, not working, not receiving benefits, not in apprenticeship)
- However, you as the claimant don't meet the UK residency requirement

**For Blake (age 2):** Not eligible  
- Blake meets all the child-specific criteria (right age, not working, not receiving benefits, not in apprenticeship)
- However, you as the claimant don't meet the UK residency requirement

**Overall:** Both children would otherwise qualify, but the UK residency requirement for claimants means you cannot claim Child Benefit for either child at this time.

If your situation changes and you move back to the UK or obtain the relevant immigration status, you would be able to claim Child Benefit for both children at that point. 

Thank you for your understanding, and I wish you and your family all the best!

reasonings: Alex (age 10) meets the child qualification criteria: under 16 years old, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements, which is a mandatory condition for claiming Child Benefit. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex.

Blake (age 2) meets the child qualification criteria: under 16 years old, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements, which is a mandatory condition for claiming Child Benefit. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children, Alex (age 10) and Blake (age 2), both of whom would otherwise qualify for Child Benefit based on their ages and circumstances. However, the claimant lives abroad and does not meet any of the special circumstance criteria (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying conditions, Crown servant, etc.). Since UK residency is a mandatory requirement for claiming Child Benefit, the claimant is ineligible to claim for either child.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
